title: `Function::EraseArgument` and deserialization exception
description:
### What happens?

Hi DuckDB Team,

I’m encountering an assertion failure when using DuckDB 1.4.0 with the `datasketches` extension.

### Error

```
Serialization Error: Error during bind of function in deserialization: 
Assertion triggered in file "/Users/rusty/Development/all-extensions/extensions/datasketches/duckdb/src/function/function.cpp" on line 176: 
arguments.size() == bound_function.arguments.size()
```

### Relevant Code

The assertion is in `Function::EraseArgument`:

```cpp
void Function::EraseArgument(SimpleFunction &bound_function, vector<unique_ptr<Expression>> &arguments,
                             idx_t argument_index) {
    if (bound_function.original_arguments.empty()) {
        bound_function.original_arguments = bound_function.arguments;
    }
>>>    D_ASSERT(arguments.size() == bound_function.arguments.size());
    D_ASSERT(argument_index < arguments.size());
    arguments.erase_at(argument_index);
    bound_function.arguments.erase_at(argument_index);
}
```

### What I’m Doing

In the `datasketches` extension, I call `Function::EraseArgument` to strip out constant arguments from aggregate functions. Example:
[https://github.com/Query-farm/datasketches/blob/916aaf1a63d56a3384002222d54196360bb0bdb4/src/generated.cpp#L66](https://github.com/Query-farm/datasketches/blob/916aaf1a63d56a3384002222d54196360bb0bdb4/src/generated.cpp#L66)

* I define the aggregate function with arguments `{LogicalType::INTEGER, LogicalType::INTEGER}`.
* In the bind function, I check if the first argument is constant. If so, I move it into the bind data and remove the argument via `Function::EraseArgument`.

### My Question

This seems to imply that I need to provide an **additional definition** of the function with `{LogicalType::INTEGER}` (after the constant is removed).

* Is this the correct approach?
* What if no sensible default exists for the “removed” argument—should I still define the alternate signature?

### How to Reproduce

You can trigger the exception by:

```sql
SELECT datasketch_quantiles(16, 5.0::float);
```

on a debug build of DuckDB with the `datasketches` extension.
Alternatively, run `test_debug` in the `datasketches` repo.

Thanks for clarifying the right way to handle this!

Cheers,
Rusty


### To Reproduce

See above

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_data (id, value, description) VALUES (1, 5.0, 'Test entry 1'), (2, NULL, 'Test entry 2'), (3, 10.5, 'Test entry 3');
SELECT datasketch_quantiles(value, CAST(id AS FLOAT)) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (ref_id INTEGER, amount INTEGER NOT NULL CHECK (amount >= 0));
INSERT INTO additional_data (ref_id, amount) SELECT id, CAST(value AS INTEGER) FROM test_data WHERE description LIKE 'Test%';
